mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) over several months reflects expanding real economic output, which directly boosts corporate earnings and leads to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO 3-month average and subsequent 60-day SPX return is below 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals a restrictive monetary stance that raises discount rates on equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in FEDFUNDS>4.5% periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad consumer and investment demand strength that reliably boosts corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a logistic regression of 20-day forward return direction on HOUST and RSAFS changes is not statistically significant (p>0.05) over any 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion regime (unemployment rate below 4% and rising), labor market tightening signals impending recession, so a rise in UNRATE over two consecutive months leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises 0.2pp or more over 2 months
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE below 4% and rising, the 20-day forward SPX return is not negative on average (one-sided t-test p>0.10), or the signal degradation does not accelerate in out-of-sample tests after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change on 20-day SPX returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below the index base of 100, it signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and possible recession, which depresses corporate earnings and results in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day SPX return when INDPRO<100 is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p>0.05) over the entire available history.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3% and the fed funds rate remains below that inflation rate, negative real interest rates emerge that incentivize borrowing and speculation in equities, pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY
failure_condition: The 60-day SPX return during negative real rate periods is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10), or the logistic regression interaction term is insignificant over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery regime, a sharp monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS > 1% MoM) signals a consumption-led rebound that rapidly lifts investor sentiment and equity prices, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day SPX return following RSAFS MoM>1% in post-recession periods (as defined by NBER) is not positive at the 5% significance level, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 out-of-sample.
